purpose: File-level guide for the WEEX monitor skill.

scripts/weex_monitor_cli.py.role: Primary deterministic monitor CLI for task normalization, monitor confirmation text/token binding, SQLite local persistence, append-only events, confirmed-task lookup, live position revalidation, price TP/SL body generation/submission through trader guard, PnL trigger evaluation, dry-run checks, live loop execution, delegated live PnL close execution, failure review state, and execution claim locking.
when: creating or previewing a monitor DSL
rendering confirmation text and a confirmation token before activation
saving an active monitor task after explicit monitor confirmation and matching confirmation token
building a WEEX TP/SL conditional order body for a price monitor
evaluating a PnL monitor from normalized position snapshots
listing or cancelling local monitor tasks
running a dry-run trigger pass against mock positions
running a bounded dry-run loop against mock position sequences
running a confirmed live loop for active PnL monitors
building a non-mutating live delegate summary for weex-trader-skill
submitting a confirmed and live-position-validated price TP/SL task through weex-trader-skill with --confirm-live
running a confirmed-token-validated live PnL check that delegates market close to weex-trader-skill
recording delegated live failures as review_required

SKILL.md.role: Behavioral routing, supported MVP scenarios, and safety policy for the monitor skill.

file-index.json.role: This navigation guide; keep it updated when the skill surface, docs, tests, or scripts change.

tests/test_weex_monitor_cli.py.role: Behavioral tests for monitor task normalization, confirmation token binding, dry-run and live loop execution, live delegation, failure recording, execution claim locking, and CLI subprocess flows.

tests/test_docs_consistency.py.role: Documentation and package consistency tests for frontmatter, manifest identity, safety-boundary text, AI natural-language DSL rules, file-index coverage, and prohibited trader internal imports.
